sections.label: THINKING
sections.content: The MOUSE action changed the board (board_changed: true) but the level is not completed. Let me check what changed - did the agent move? Let me compare the before and after frames to see what happened.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check what changed after MOUSE click
seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']

# Find the agent
agent = None
for n in nodes:
    if n['color'] == 'b':
        agent = n
        break

if agent:
    boundary = agent['boundary']
    rows = [p[0] for p in boundary]
    cols = [p[1] for p in boundary]
    print(f"Agent pos: rows {min(rows)}-{max(rows)}, cols {min(cols)}-{max(cols)}")

# Check previous frame agent position
prev_seg = previous_frame.segmentation
prev_nodes = prev_seg['nodes']
prev_agent = None
for n in prev_nodes:
    if n['color'] == 'b':
        prev_agent = n
        break

if prev_agent:
    boundary = prev_agent['boundary']
    rows = [p[0] for p in boundary]
    cols = [p[1] for p in boundary]
    print(f"Prev agent pos: rows {min(rows)}-{max(rows)}, cols {min(cols)}-{max(cols)}")

# Count nodes to see if anything changed
print(f"Current nodes: {len(nodes)}, Prev nodes: {len(prev_nodes)}")

# Look for any changes in colors or positions
for n in nodes:
    if n['color'] not in ['b', 'B', 'S', 'g']:  # Skip background/common colors
        print(f"Node color={n['color']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, boundary={n['boundary'][:3]}...")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
